steps.description: Rust integration test for Lock flow
steps.command: cd src/api/api && SQLX_OFFLINE=true cargo test --test sequence_e2e_test seq1_lock -- --nocapture
steps.expected: Test exits with code 0; stdout confirms: lock_id issued, SR_0 computed via SHA3-256("QS_LOCK_V1" || chain_id || asset || amount || dest_addr || expiry || nonce || pk_dilithium), and lockWithSR0 transaction submitted to L1 Vault at 0x6F889C00a5e674ab0b9403AfBa0fBEbe30511c67; no FAILED or panicked lines present

steps.description: Playwright integration spec for Consumer Lock
steps.command: cd src/frontend/web && npx playwright test e2e/integration/lock.integration.spec.ts --reporter=json
steps.expected: All Playwright tests pass (0 failed); JSON reporter output contains a passed result for the lock creation flow; UI reaches success state and a corresponding locks row is observable in the database

steps.description: Verify lock row was created and pk_dilithium populated
steps.command: psql "$DATABASE_URL" -t -A -c "SELECT lock_id, status, length(pk_dilithium) FROM locks ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;"
steps.expected: Exactly one row is returned; status is either 'pending' or 'locked'; length(pk_dilithium) is exactly 1952 bytes (Dilithium public key size); lock_id is non-null and non-empty

steps.description: Verify totalLocked increased on Sepolia Vault
steps.expected: Returned uint256 value is strictly greater than 0 wei, confirming at least one Deposit was accepted by the L1 Vault and the Locked event was emitted; command exits with code 0

acceptance_criteria: Backend integration test (seq1_lock) exits with code 0 and stdout confirms SR_0 was computed and lockWithSR0 was submitted to L1
Playwright lock.integration.spec.ts reports 0 failed tests and the UI lock creation flow reaches a success state
The most recent row in the locks table has status equal to 'locked' or 'pending' — not null, not error
The most recent row in the locks table has length(pk_dilithium) equal to exactly 1952 bytes
cast call to Sepolia Vault totalLocked() returns a value strictly greater than 0 wei
No hardcoded bypass, stub response, or silent fallback is present in the backend trace — the Dilithium signature must be verified and nonce must be checked before lock_id is issued
The L1 Vault address used in all steps resolves to 0x6F889C00a5e674ab0b9403AfBa0fBEbe30511c67 (canonical Sepolia deployment per SEQUENCES.md §Architecture)

Error: HTTP error 522 with body: sepolia.org | 522: Connection timed out

Connection timed out Error code 522

Visit cloudflare.com for more information.
2026-04-30 08:52:03 UTC
You

Browser

Working
Ashburn

Cloudflare

Working
rpc.sepolia.org

Host

Error

What happened?

The initial connection between Cloudflare's network and the origin web server timed out. As a result, the web page can not be displayed.

What can I do?

If you're a visitor of this website:

Please try again in a few minutes.

If you're the owner of this website:

Contact your hosting provider letting them know your web server is not completing requests. An Error 522 means that the request was able to connect to your web server, but that the request didn't finish. The most likely cause is that something on your server is hogging resources. Additional troubleshooting information here.

Cloudflare Ray ID: 9f454e65f8ab81fd • Your IP: 145.132.103.19 • Performance & security by Cloudflare

HTTP diagnostics: cf-ray: 9f454e65f8ab81fd-IAD server: cloudflare

observations: Exit code 101 indicates test failure
3 tests passed: test_lock_creates_successfully, test_lock_status_retrievable both OK
1 test FAILED: test_lock_rejects_invalid_signature
Panic detected at src/api/api/tests/sequence_e2e_test.rs:218:9 with message: 'Invalid signature should be rejected, got 200'
The test expected HTTP 400/401 rejection for invalid signature but received 200 OK
stdout shows 'test result: FAILED. 3 passed; 1 failed'
Lock creation and status retrieval work correctly (SR_0 computed, lock_id issued, pending status set)
Multiple unused variable warnings in stderr (body variables), severity=low
Signature validation endpoint is broken: accepts invalid signatures when it should reject them

issues.title: Signature validation not enforced
issues.detail: test_lock_rejects_invalid_signature failed because invalid signatures are accepted (HTTP 200) instead of being rejected. This is a critical security flaw in the lock endpoint that violates the Quantum Shield protocol requirement to validate dilithium signatures.

issues.title: Test assertion failure
issues.detail: Panic at line 218 confirms endpoint did not validate the invalid signature and returned 200 instead of error status. This causes the entire sequence test to fail.

issues.title: Unused variable with unwrap_or_default pattern
issues.detail: Multiple instances of 'let body: Value = resp.json().await.unwrap_or_default()' at lines 671, 1217, 1235, 1479, 1499, 1517. Pattern silently swallows JSON parsing errors.

observations: Total tests: 40, passed: 40, failed: 0, skipped: 0
Exit code: 0
JSON reporter stats: expected=40, unexpected=0, flaky=0
Test suites: Mobile Chrome and Mobile Safari both passed all scenarios

Key scenarios validated: lock creation with valid SR_0 and lock_id, lock persistence in DB, explorer integration, nonce reuse rejection, expiration rejection, missing field validation, multi-lock distinctness
Lock creation flow confirmed: lock_id and sr_0 generated correctly, SMT proof generated (17030 chars)
Database persistence confirmed: lock visible via GET /v1/explorer/locks with active status
Explorer integration confirmed: totalLocks count incremented after lock creation
All edge cases tested: nonce reuse (409), expired requests (400), missing fields (422)
psql exited with code 0 (success)
Exactly one row returned as expected
lock_id is non-null and non-empty: 0x5f846e6c90c9230bdbbe5c13ea0c93ff79d0d9bd841678108e66e8d15e995a64
status value is 'pending' (acceptable per spec)
pk_dilithium length is 32 bytes, not 1952 bytes

issues.title: Dilithium public key truncated or corrupted
issues.detail: Expected length(pk_dilithium) = 1952 bytes (standard Dilithium public key size), but got 32 bytes. This indicates either the cryptographic key was not written correctly, was truncated during storage, or is using an incompatible key format. The cryptographic write path is compromised.

observations: cast invoked against legitimate L1Vault address 0x07012aeF87C6E423c32F2f8eaF81762f63337260
Command exited with code 1 (non-zero)
RPC endpoint (rpc.sepolia.org) returned HTTP 522 Connection timed out
No contract state data retrieved; stdout empty
Infrastructure failure (Cloudflare), not application logic failure

issues.title: RPC endpoint unavailable
issues.detail: rpc.sepolia.org returned HTTP 522 (Connection timed out). The origin server failed to respond within Cloudflare's timeout window. This is a transient infrastructure issue, not a contract or sequence defect.

quality_findings.title: Signature validation completely bypassed — invalid signatures accepted with HTTP 200
quality_findings.detail: The lock endpoint accepts requests carrying an invalid Dilithium signature and returns HTTP 200 OK instead of rejecting them. This is not merely a test assertion failure: it means any attacker can submit a lock request with a forged or malformed signature and the backend will process it as legitimate. The backend layer test panicked with 'Invalid signature should be rejected, got 200' (line 218), and the frontend layer tests all passed — meaning the frontend integration tests never exercised the invalid-signature path, leaving the production endpoint silently open. This violates the core Quantum Shield protocol invariant that every lock must carry a valid post-quantum signature before state is mutated.
evidence: backend stdout: 'SEQ#1-03 Invalid sig response: status=200'
backend stderr: 'thread seq1_lock::test_lock_rejects_invalid_signature panicked at src/api/api/tests/sequence_e2e_test.rs:218:9: Invalid signature should be rejected, got 200'
frontend stats: expected=40, unexpected=0 — frontend test suite contains no invalid-signature test, so the bypass is invisible to it
Cross-layer: backend FAIL (exit 101) + frontend PASS (exit 0) = silent production exposure

quality_findings.title: Dilithium public key stored as 32 bytes instead of 1952 bytes — cryptographic key truncated
quality_findings.detail: The DB layer query returned `length(pk_dilithium) = 32` for the most recently created lock. The standard Dilithium2 public key is 1312 bytes; Dilithium3 is 1952 bytes; neither is 32 bytes. 32 bytes is the size of a SHA-256 hash or a bytes32 field — strongly suggesting the code is either (a) storing only a hash/commitment of the key rather than the key itself, (b) calling `hex_to_bytes32_or_zero` (silent-failure pattern #4) and truncating the key to a 32-byte zero-padded value, or (c) writing to the wrong column. Because the signature validation endpoint already fails to reject invalid signatures (finding above), and the stored public key is not a valid Dilithium key, the system cannot perform any real post-quantum verification at any layer. The combination of these two bugs means the entire cryptographic security guarantee of the Consumer Lock flow is absent.
evidence: db stdout: '0x5f846e6c90c9230bdbbe5c13ea0c93ff79d0d9bd841678108e66e8d15e995a64|pending|32'
Expected pk_dilithium length: 1312–1952 bytes (Dilithium2/3); actual: 32 bytes
Cross-layer: frontend reports 'smt_proof length=17030 chars' confirming a large proof is generated, yet the public key stored is only 32 bytes — proof data and key data are inconsistent
Silent-failure pattern #4 (hex_to_bytes32_or_zero) is a known regression target for this codebase

quality_findings.title: DB status recorded as 'pending' but frontend explorer shows lock as 'active' — state inconsistency
quality_findings.detail: The DB layer query against the most recent lock row returns `status = pending`. However, the frontend integration test for 'lock is persisted in DB and visible via GET /v1/explorer/locks' logs '[Lock Persisted] lock_id=..., status=active'. These are different rows (different lock_ids), but both were created during the same test run without any intervening release/confirmation step. If the explorer endpoint is reading a derived or cached status field rather than the canonical DB column, or if the status transition logic promotes some locks to 'active' and not others non-deterministically, this is a silent data-consistency failure. The spec (SEQUENCES.md §1) must clarify whether newly created locks are 'pending' or 'active'; if 'pending' is correct, the explorer API is returning stale/wrong state.
evidence: db stdout: '...0x5f846e6c90c9230bdbbe5c13ea0c93ff79d0d9bd841678108e66e8d15e995a64|pending|32'
frontend stdout: '[Lock Persisted] lock_id=0x9b96d27ef79df10d3b056d8912bf97fbb770ec8772d905791f293a30bf9edd72, status=active'
Both locks created in the same test run with no explicit state-transition step between creation and the explorer query
Cross-layer: DB says 'pending', explorer API says 'active' for a freshly created lock

quality_findings.title: unwrap_or_default() on response JSON silently swallows parse errors in 6 test locations
quality_findings.detail: Six locations in sequence_e2e_test.rs use the pattern `let body: Value = resp.json().await.unwrap_or_default()` and then never use `body`. This matches silent-failure pattern #3 from the high-priority regression list. If the server returns a non-JSON body (e.g., an HTML error page, a 500 with plain text, or a truncated response), `unwrap_or_default()` silently substitutes `Value::Null` and the test continues without ever inspecting the response body. Tests that should catch malformed server responses will pass green while the server is broken. The `body` variable being unused (compiler warnings at lines 671, 1217, 1235, 1479, 1499, 1517) compounds this: even if parsing succeeded, nothing asserts on the content.
evidence: backend stderr: 'warning: unused variable: `body`' at sequence_e2e_test.rs:671, 1217, 1235, 1479, 1499, 1517
Pattern: 'let body: Value = resp.json().await.unwrap_or_default()' — matches known silent-failure pattern #3
6 separate occurrences across the test file, suggesting a copy-paste anti-pattern propagation

quality_findings.title: L1 Vault state unverifiable — lock accepted by backend+DB but on-chain totalLocked unconfirmable
quality_findings.detail: The L1 layer failed with HTTP 522 (RPC endpoint timeout), so it is impossible to confirm whether the on-chain Vault at 0x07012aeF87C6E423c32F2f8eaF81762f63337260 actually recorded the lock. The frontend reported 'totalLocks: 33 -> 34', but this counter is served by the backend/explorer, not read directly from the chain. If the backend increments an off-chain counter independently of the on-chain call (or if the on-chain call is fire-and-forget with `Ok(())` swallowing errors — silent-failure pattern #5), the UI can show a successful lock increment while the Vault state never moved. The RPC failure prevents ruling this out for this run.
evidence: l1 stderr: 'Error: HTTP error 522 with body: ... sepolia.org | 522: Connection timed out'
frontend stdout: '[Explorer] totalLocks: 33 -> 34' — counter incremented in explorer without L1 confirmation
l1 stdout: '' (empty) — no on-chain value retrieved
Cross-layer: frontend PASS + L1 FAIL = on-chain state unverified for this run

fixer_proposals.title: Enforce Dilithium signature validation in the lock endpoint
fixer_proposals.rationale: Addresses the two critical findings: 'Signature validation not enforced' and 'Signature validation completely bypassed — invalid signatures accepted with HTTP 200'. The lock handler must verify the submitted Dilithium signature against the stored public key before mutating any state. The fix adds a mandatory verification step that returns HTTP 400/401 on failure, making test_lock_rejects_invalid_signature pass and closing the security gap.

fixer_proposals.title: Store the full Dilithium public key instead of a 32-byte hash
fixer_proposals.rationale: Addresses the critical finding 'Dilithium public key stored as 32 bytes instead of 1952 bytes'. The DB write path is truncating or hashing the key to 32 bytes. This fix ensures the complete raw key bytes are written to the pk_dilithium BYTEA column, and adds a post-write assertion in tests to detect regressions.

fixer_proposals.title: Fix unwrap_or_default() on response JSON in tests — propagate parse errors
fixer_proposals.rationale: Addresses the high-severity finding 'unwrap_or_default() on response JSON silently swallows parse errors in 6 test locations'. Replaces every silent-fallback parse with an explicit .expect() or explicit error propagation so test failures surface instead of silently passing with a null body. Also removes the dead binding where body is unused.

fixer_proposals.title: Investigation required — DB status 'pending' vs explorer 'active' state inconsistency
fixer_proposals.rationale: Addresses the high-severity finding 'DB status recorded as pending but frontend explorer shows lock as active'. Two locks created in the same test run show different statuses across layers. The root cause is ambiguous: either (a) the explorer reads a derived/cached status field not backed by the canonical DB column, (b) there is a non-deterministic state-transition side-effect triggered on some creation paths but not others, or (c) the spec in SEQUENCES.md §1 is inconsistent with the implementation. A code-level fix cannot be safely proposed without first identifying which lock_id the explorer is querying and how the status column is populated. This must be investigated before an automatic patch is applied.

fixer_proposals.title: Investigation required — L1 on-chain lock confirmation not verifiable due to fire-and-forget RPC call
fixer_proposals.rationale: Addresses the medium-severity finding 'L1 Vault state unverifiable — lock accepted by backend+DB but on-chain totalLocked unconfirmable'. The RPC timeout prevented verification of whether the backend call to the L1 Vault is awaited or fire-and-forget. If the backend uses an error-swallowing pattern (silent-failure pattern #5: Ok(()) on a failed on-chain call), the UI counter can increment while the chain never moved. A concrete diff requires locating the exact call site that submits the on-chain transaction and confirming whether its Result is propagated or discarded. Without access to that path, proposing a patch risks introducing incorrect error handling in the contract interaction layer.

final_verdict.summary: The Consumer Lock sequence has two critical cryptographic failures that together eliminate all post-quantum security guarantees: (1) the lock endpoint accepts requests carrying invalid Dilithium signatures and returns HTTP 200 instead of rejecting them, and (2) the Dilithium public key is stored as only 32 bytes instead of the required 1952 bytes, indicating the key is being truncated or hashed before persistence. These are not test harness issues — the production endpoint is provably bypassing signature enforcement, and the stored key material is not a valid Dilithium key. Additionally, the DB layer agent reports pk_dilithium length=32 while the spec mandates exactly 1952 bytes, and the L1 RPC is unreachable (HTTP 522) making on-chain state unverifiable; the frontend passing all 40 tests provides no cover because it never exercised the invalid-signature code path. No fixer proposals were received, so auto-apply is not possible; human review and production-safe fixes are required before merge.
must_fix_before_merge: Signature validation bypassed: lock endpoint accepts invalid Dilithium signatures with HTTP 200 — enforce signature verification before any state mutation
Dilithium public key truncated to 32 bytes: pk_dilithium must be stored as full 1952-byte Dilithium3 key — identify and fix the write path (likely hex_to_bytes32_or_zero truncation)
DB acceptance criterion violated: pk_dilithium length must equal exactly 1952 bytes per spec and acceptance criteria
status inconsistency between DB layer ('pending') and explorer API ('active') for freshly created locks — clarify canonical state machine and fix the inconsistent field
Six test locations use unwrap_or_default() on response JSON and discard the body variable — replace with explicit assertions to prevent silent parse-error masking
L1 RPC endpoint (rpc.sepolia.org) returning HTTP 522: rerun L1 verification against a healthy RPC endpoint to confirm on-chain totalLocked > 0 and lockWithSR0 submission
unresolved_questions: Is pk_dilithium being truncated to 32 bytes by a hex_to_bytes32_or_zero call, or is it storing only a commitment/hash of the key? The code path must be audited to determine the exact write site.

Does the lock endpoint have any signature validation code that is conditionally compiled out, feature-flagged off, or short-circuited by a middleware bypass? The HTTP 200 response to an invalid signature suggests validation is either absent or skipped entirely.

The DB layer reports the most recent lock as status='pending' while the frontend explorer API reports a different lock_id (created in the same run) as status='active': is 'active' a legitimate initial state for some code path, or is the explorer reading a stale/cached/derived field? This must be resolved against SEQUENCES.md §1.

The L1 layer queried address 0x07012aeF87C6E423c32F2f8eaF81762f63337260 but the canonical Vault address per the spec and acceptance criteria is 0x6F889C00a5e674ab0b9403AfBa0fBEbe30511c67 — these differ; the test plan's L1 step and the spec address must be reconciled before the L1 verification can be considered valid.
No fixer proposals were received ('awaiting parallel fixer output'): are fixes in progress, and if so, do any touch src/contracts/l1/ or migrations?
